message:
chore(beads): add FUSE E2E and gate-validation issues, update proptest close reason

Add three new beads issues tracking work done in this session:
- bd-247a: Add FUSE E2E test for setattr chmod and statfs (in_progress)
- bd-3aad: Run rch-offloaded core gates (fmt/check/clippy) and triage
  failures (closed -- all gates pass)
- bd-g6sl: Validate unignored lightweight fuse_e2e tests and stabilize
  failures (closed -- 10/0 pass rate confirmed)

Update bd-2v80 (proptest property-based tests for ffs-inode) with
revised timestamps and expanded close reason detailing the 8 new
proptests added and the 39-test pass count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -145,6 +145,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-23n","title":"CLI: Implement mount subcommand (ffs-cli mount <image> <mountpoint>)","description":"Goal: provide the primary entry point for users: mount an ext4 image via FUSE.

Deliverables:
- Add CLI parsing for mount args + options (read-only, allow-other, etc).
- Use ffs-core OpenFs API to open/validate image.
- Construct ffs-fuse Filesystem + start fuser mount loop.

Acceptance:
- Manual E2E: mount fixture image read-only and run ls/stat/cat.
- Errors are user-friendly and map to correct errno/log messages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:29.600373001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:51:15.997299182Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:51:15.997281579Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23n","depends_on_id":"bd-27a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:05.615727710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-23n","depends_on_id":"bd-3is","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:05.699859913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-23n","depends_on_id":"bd-3vn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:05.526795366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-23r","title":"TUI: Minimal dashboard (cache/MVCC/repair metrics) via ftui [phased]","description":"Goal: provide a live view of internal state for debugging and demos.

Deliverables:
- A ftui-based screen that shows:
  - cache hit/miss, p value
  - mvcc commit seq, open txns
  - repair overhead + recent scrub events

Acceptance:
- Can run against a local mount or harness run and update periodically.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PinkCreek","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:50.041190926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:42:44.518865708Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:42:44.518843938Z","close_reason":"Implemented: Dashboard model + DashboardSnapshot DTO + 3-panel view (cache/MVCC/scrub) + 12 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23r","depends_on_id":"bd-2t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:06.040436183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-23r","depends_on_id":"bd-ece","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:05.953356393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-23s","title":"Epic: ext4 Advanced Semantics (htree, symlink, xattr, permissions)","description":"Follow-on work to reach stronger ext4 parity beyond the MVP.

Scope examples:
- htree indexed dirs
- symlinks
- xattrs
- permission bits and ownership mapping details

Acceptance:
- Each feature has fixtures and at least one conformance comparison against kernel behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:26:58.089326744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:27:48.847862449Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:27:48.847835960Z","close_reason":"All 4 sub-tasks complete: permissions/rdev (bd-q0g), xattr (bd-3bu), symlinks (bd-wse), htree (bd-3qy).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23s","depends_on_id":"bd-3bu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:52.066658739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-23s","depends_on_id":"bd-3qy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:51.896047591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-23s","depends_on_id":"bd-q0g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:52.155086654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-23s","depends_on_id":"bd-wse","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:51.978648257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-247a","title":"Add FUSE E2E test for setattr chmod and statfs","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:28:43.465219912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:28:46.725319535Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-25k","title":"ext4: Implement group descriptor checksum verification hooks (metadata_csum)","description":"Goal: prepare for ext4 metadata checksum verification (CRC32C) when the filesystem uses metadata_csum.

Deliverables:
- Determine checksum algorithm and seed for group descriptors (from legacy ext4 code) including how group number participates.
- Add a function: verify_group_desc_checksum(desc_bytes, group_nr, superblock) -> Result<()>.
- If metadata_csum is not enabled, skip verification.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests: known-good descriptor passes; flipped-bit fails.
- Error type is stable and mapped correctly for user-facing surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:17:25.017305716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:44.617157237Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:44.617138091Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25k","depends_on_id":"bd-1a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.845984197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25k","depends_on_id":"bd-28h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:02.431265758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25k","depends_on_id":"bd-2cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.766942705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-25zj","title":"Harden extent header tail_off calculation with checked_mul to prevent overflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-23T05:40:53.152281354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-23T05:44:06.406447365Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T05:44:06.406424422Z","close_reason":"Used checked_mul/checked_add for extent header tail_off calculation to prevent potential overflow with malicious eh_max values","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-265","title":"ffs-block: Concurrency design for cache (sharding + no I/O under lock)","description":"Goal: make sure cache remains safe and fast under many concurrent FUSE operations.

Deliverables:
- Decide locking strategy (single Mutex vs sharded) and document it.
- Ensure we never do disk I/O while holding a global cache lock.
- Add a concurrency stress test under asupersync lab runtime (deterministic schedule) or standard threads with timeouts.

Acceptance:
- Stress test does not deadlock.
- Contention is measured (optional) and stays reasonable under synthetic load.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:00.946703440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:53:29.955924247Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:53:29.955906063Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-265","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.057034396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -249,7 +250,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ugt","title":"FUSE write-path E2E tests for ext4","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:57:38.096077272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:08:07.352962265Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:08:07.352939422Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","testing"],"comments":[{"id":280,"issue_id":"bd-2ugt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CalmBridge: Added 11 write-path FUSE E2E tests to crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs covering: create+read, overwrite, mkdir+nested create, unlink, rmdir, rename (same dir + cross-dir), hard link (with inode verification), symlink (create+readlink+follow), setattr/truncate, and large file write (64KiB multi-block). All gated with #[ignore = "requires /dev/fuse"]. Compiles clean, clippy clean, full workspace tests pass (exit=0).","created_at":"2026-02-22T07:27:42Z"},{"id":283,"issue_id":"bd-2ugt","author":"SunnyElm","text":"Completed ext4 FUSE write-path E2E coverage in crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs. Tightened write semantics by making fuse_write_overwrite_and_append actually append via OpenOptions::append + write_all and validating final payload; added fuse_rmdir_non_empty_fails asserting DirectoryNotEmpty; strengthened fuse_hard_link with nlink=2 assertions on both paths. Existing ext4 write-path ignored E2E set now covers create/read, overwrite+append, mkdir+nested create, unlink, rmdir empty+non-empty error, rename (same/cross-dir), hard links, symlink create/follow, setattr truncate, and large-file write/readback. Gates run: cargo fmt --check; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ffs-target cargo check --workspace --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ffs-target cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ffs-target cargo test -p ffs-harness --test fuse_e2e -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ffs-target cargo test -p ffs-harness (all passing in this environment, FUSE tests remain ignored by design unless explicitly enabled). UBS: ubs --only=rust crates/ffs-harness still reports baseline test-focused critical/warning inventory (not introduced by this change).","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:08:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uj","title":"Track: Performance Baselines + Regression Gates","description":"Establish measurable performance goals and prevent regressions via baselines + profiling discipline (per extreme-software-optimization).\n\nAcceptance: (1) hyperfine baselines exist for key paths (detect/open/parse ext4 superblock+groupdesc, btrfs superblock+map), (2) benchmark harness exists for cache hot paths, (3) optimization PRs require before/after numbers + isomorphism proof + golden checksums.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:32.851180914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:32:05.682177354Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:32:05.682151305Z","close_reason":"All 3 sub-tasks complete: criterion benches (bd-3ff), golden/isomorphism proofs (bd-p1l), benchmark baselines (bd-37g).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uj","depends_on_id":"bd-37g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:02.651808806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uj","depends_on_id":"bd-3ff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:02.817592180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uj","depends_on_id":"bd-p1l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:02.735713072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-2uj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization is not a vibe.

We follow the loop:
- baseline (hyperfine)
- profile
- change one lever
- prove behavior unchanged (goldens + checksums)
- re-measure

This track exists to keep performance claims honest and regressions detectable.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2v0n","title":"Replace lossy as-casts with try_from in groups_count and alloc (ffs-ondisk, ffs-alloc)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:46:13.154417051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:59:25.473104631Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T02:59:25.473084493Z","close_reason":"Fixed: groups_count uses try_from with unwrap_or cap, absolute_to_group_block uses try_from for group number","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

All 39 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2v90","title":"Track ffs-demo binary command-contract hardening","description":"Post-closure hardening slice to explicitly support and validate the command contract: cargo run --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing. Verify bin target wiring, binary invocation test coverage, E2E script phase coverage, README command, and rch-based validation evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueElm","created_at":"2026-02-19T00:31:51.922469754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T00:35:10.265556649Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T00:35:10.265528446Z","close_reason":"Completed: command-contract hardening tracked and validated (rch check/test/run all green)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo","e2e"],"comments":[{"id":263,"issue_id":"bd-2v90","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation evidence (cargo-heavy all offloaded via rch):\n1) rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-repair --all-targets -> exit 0\n2) rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-repair --test self_healing_demo_e2e -- --nocapture -> exit 0 (all tests passed, including demo_binary_runs_via_self_healing_command)\n3) rch exec -- cargo run -p ffs-repair --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing -> exit 0 with expected 6-line summary and PASS\n\nScope validated for explicit command contract hardening:\n- ffs-demo bin target wiring\n- binary-path integration test execution\n- e2e script explicit bin command phase\n- README quick example alignment\n\nNo additional code changes required from my side; validation + traceability complete for this tracking bead.","created_at":"2026-02-19T00:35:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vfd","title":"Honor MountConfig.unmount_timeout in MountHandle::wait","description":"MountHandle::wait currently polls shutdown forever and ignores MountConfig.unmount_timeout. Implement timeout-aware wait behavior and add tests to verify bounded wait without shutdown signal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:48.336743208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:53:37.536481679Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:53:37.536459488Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","mount"],"comments":[{"id":289,"issue_id":"bd-2vfd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MountHandle::wait now honors MountConfig.unmount_timeout: it exits the wait loop on timeout and forces unmount, instead of polling forever. Added mount_handle_wait_respects_unmount_timeout regression test and kept shutdown-driven wait behavior covered. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p ffs-fuse mount_handle_wait_returns_on_shutdown; cargo test -p ffs-fuse mount_handle_wait_respects_unmount_timeout; cargo check/clippy -p ffs-fuse --all-targets; cargo test -p ffs-fuse.","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:53:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vt","title":"btrfs: Expand superblock parsing (sys_chunk_array + validation)","description":"Goal: parse enough of btrfs superblock to bootstrap mapping without reading chunk tree yet.

Deliverables:
- Parse sys_chunk_array and sys_chunk_array_size fields.
- Add validation for sectorsize/nodesize/stripesize: non-zero, power-of-two where required, and sane bounds.
- Expose a helper to iterate sys_chunk_array entries.

Acceptance:
- Fixture-backed test: parse a real btrfs superblock and confirm sys_chunk_array size/contents are plausible.
- No panics on malformed input.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:36.214652115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:04:40.654597651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:04:40.654575529Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ondisk"]}

 {"id":"bd-39ip","title":"ffs-alloc: harden error paths and boundary conditions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T21:59:32.211606958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:09:29.908215564Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T22:09:29.908197340Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-39km","title":"Add baseline unit tests for ffs-ext4 facade re-exports","description":"ffs-ext4 currently has no unit tests and is a thin re-export facade over ffs-ondisk ext4 APIs. Add small compile/runtime smoke tests that validate key ext4 symbols/constants/functions are exported and callable, without introducing new behavior or dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:32:19.789542061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:54:02.634011080Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:54:02.633989649Z","close_reason":"Added ffs-ext4 facade smoke tests for exported constants/checksum helper and parser callability; validated with rch-offloaded check/clippy/test (4 tests passing).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3a3g","title":"Add ffs-core mutation path tests (create/write/unlink error handling)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:32:28.532383755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T21:04:24.337951035Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T21:04:24.337933001Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 mutation-path error tests for create/write/unlink handling in ffs-core and validated via rch-targeted check/clippy/tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3aad","title":"Run rch-offloaded core gates (fmt/check/clippy) and triage failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:21:41.558043416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:25:20.972284620Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:25:20.972262860Z","close_reason":"All core gates pass via rch (fmt/check/clippy)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3ajn","title":"Replace FUSE offset conversion fallbacks (unwrap_or MAX/0) with proper error propagation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-23T05:41:01.216300887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-23T05:54:52.654777929Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T05:54:52.654757030Z","close_reason":"Replaced FUSE i64->u64 offset fallbacks (unwrap_or(0)) in read, readdir, and write handlers with proper EINVAL error replies for negative offsets. Remaining unwrap_or patterns in the file are either mathematically safe (usize->u64 on 64-bit) or defensible saturation (u64->i64 for dir entry offsets, metrics recording).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3b3s","title":"Join shutdown-trigger thread in mount_handle_wait_returns_on_shutdown","description":"UBS flagged detached thread lifecycle in ffs-fuse tests. mount_handle_wait_returns_on_shutdown spawns a background thread without joining it, which can leak work across tests and cause nondeterministic behavior under load. Capture JoinHandle and join before test exit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:35:56.175028125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:39:51.255562681Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:39:51.255530080Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","fuse","tests"],"comments":[{"id":286,"issue_id":"bd-3b3s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Patched ffs-fuse test mount_handle_wait_returns_on_shutdown to capture and join the shutdown-trigger thread instead of detaching it. This removes background thread leakage/flakiness risk in test runs. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check -p ffs-fuse --all-targets; cargo clippy -p ffs-fuse --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ffs-fuse mount_handle_wait_returns_on_shutdown -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:39:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bf","title":"Docs: Resolve layering contract (ffs-fuse / ffs-core / semantics boundaries)","description":"Goal: make crate boundaries unambiguous so implementation does not drift.

Decisions to document:
- ffs-fuse is a thin adapter over an internal FsOps trait.
- ffs-core owns open/detect/validate and constructs per-format contexts.
- semantics crates (ffs-inode/ffs-dir/ffs-extent/...) implement the actual operations.

Acceptance:
- PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md + COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC agree on the layering and dependency direction.
- Cargo.toml dependency edges match the documented layering.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:00.976855586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:03:15.319842714Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:03:15.319761162Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bf","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:19.679993026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-g30","title":"Track: CLI + TUI (ffs-cli + ffs-tui)","description":"Provide operator tooling: CLI subcommands (inspect/info/fsck/mount), and a frankentui-based dashboard to observe cache/MVCC/repair stats.\n\nAcceptance: CLI is the primary UX for harness + debugging; TUI can attach to a running mount to show internal metrics.  {"id":"bd-g59h","title":"Honor BackpressureDecision::Throttle in FUSE write path","description":"FrankenFuse::should_shed currently only checks for BackpressureDecision::Shed, so Degraded/Critical throttle decisions are effectively ignored and writes proceed at full speed. Apply throttle behavior (delay) for Throttle decisions and add regression test coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:51:33.860444149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:28.420379182Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:28.420348394Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","correctness","fuse"],"comments":[{"id":288,"issue_id":"bd-g59h","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented BackpressureDecision::Throttle handling in FrankenFuse::should_shed: degraded/critical throttle now sleeps for BACKPRESSURE_THROTTLE_DELAY and proceeds instead of being ignored. Added regression test should_shed_with_degraded_gate_throttles_without_shedding. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p ffs-fuse should_shed_with_degraded_gate_throttles_without_shedding; cargo test -p ffs-fuse should_shed_with_emergency_gate_sheds_writes; cargo test -p ffs-fuse should_shed_with_normal_gate_proceeds_all; cargo check/clippy -p ffs-fuse --all-targets; cargo test -p ffs-fuse.","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:28Z"}]}
